text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 48-year-old female with history of acute myeloid leukemia (AML), FLT3-ITD positive, status post myeloablative conditioning allogeneic peripheral blood stem cell transplantation from a 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor approximately 6 months prior. History of prior splenomegaly at diagnosis with resolution on post-treatment imaging. Prior right lower lobe ground-glass opacity noted at day +90 imaging, felt to be post-infectious in etiology. Presenting for 6-month post-transplant surveillance imaging to assess for extramedullary disease recurrence, interval change in solid organ findings, and resolution of previously noted pulmonary opacity.

COMPARISON: CT chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast obtained at approximately day +90 post-transplant. CT chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast from pre-transplant workup also referenced.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following administration of 90 mL of Omnipaque 350 intravenous contrast, with imaging in the portal venous phase for the abdomen and pelvis. Axial images obtained from lung apices through the pubic symphysis with coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformations generated. No oral contrast was administered. Standard radiation dose protocol used.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and airways: The lungs are clear bilaterally. The previously identified ground-glass opacity in the right lower lobe measuring approximately 1.2 cm in greatest dimension on prior imaging has completely resolved. No residual opacity, consolidation, or nodular density is identified at that location. The remainder of both lungs shows no focal areas of consolidation, ground-glass attenuation, tree-in-bud pattern, or nodular lesions. The central airways are patent. No endobronchial lesions. The trachea is midline.

Pleura: No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pneumothorax. No pleural thickening.

Mediastinum and hila: The mediastinum is midline. No anterior, middle, or posterior mediastinal mass. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy. The hilar structures are symmetric and unremarkable; no hilar adenopathy. The thymus shows expected age-related involution. No pericardial effusion.

Heart and great vessels: The cardiac silhouette is normal in size and configuration. The aorta is normal in caliber throughout its thoracic course. No aortic aneurysm or dissection. The pulmonary vasculature is normal in caliber without evidence of pulmonary arterial hypertension or acute thromboembolic disease on this non-dedicated study.

Chest wall and osseous structures: No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions in the visualized thoracic vertebrae, ribs, clavicles, or scapulae. No soft tissue density masses involving the chest wall or paraspinal regions. No findings to suggest myeloid sarcoma involving the chest wall or epidural space.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size with a craniocaudal span of approximately 14.2 cm along the right lobe. The parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal lesion. No hypodense or hyperdense masses, nodules, or infiltrative process. No biliary ductal dilatation. The portal vein is patent with normal direction of flow inferred from enhancement pattern. Previously documented hepatomegaly (17.1 cm at initial diagnosis) has remained resolved. No capsular irregularity. No perihepatic ascites.

Spleen: The spleen measures 9.8 cm in craniocaudal dimension (normal), unchanged from 10.2 cm on day +90 post-transplant imaging and markedly diminished compared to 16.4 cm at the time of initial AML diagnosis. The splenic parenchyma enhances homogeneously without focal lesion or infarct. No perisplenic fluid.

Pancreas: The pancreas is normal in size, contour, and enhancement throughout the head, body, and tail. No pancreatic ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic fluid or inflammatory stranding.

Kidneys and adrenal glands: Both kidneys are normal in size with symmetric cortical thickness and normal enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No renal masses or cysts of concern. No perirenal stranding. Bilateral adrenal glands appear normal without thickening, nodularity, or mass.

Stomach and bowel: The stomach is unremarkable. Small and large bowel loops demonstrate normal caliber and wall thickness throughout. No bowel wall thickening, mucosal enhancement abnormality, or bowel obstruction. The appendix is not well visualized but there are no secondary signs of appendicitis. No free air.

Abdominal lymph nodes: No enlarged lymph nodes are identified in the retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or para-aortic stations. No lymphadenopathy in the porta hepatis or celiac axis regions.

Ascites and peritoneum: No ascites. No peritoneal thickening or nodularity.

Vasculature: The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. The celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and inferior mesenteric artery origins are patent without significant stenosis. No abdominal aortic aneurysm.

PELVIS:

Bladder: The urinary bladder is partially distended and normal in wall thickness and configuration.

Female reproductive organs: The uterus is normal in size and configuration. No adnexal masses identified. Small volume of physiologic free fluid at the cul-de-sac is noted.

Pelvic lymph nodes: No enlarged pelvic lymph nodes in the external iliac, internal iliac, or obturator chains. No inguinal lymphadenopathy.

Osseous structures: No lytic, blastic, or permeative osseous lesions identified in the visualized pelvis, acetabula, proximal femora, sacrum, or coccyx. No cortical disruption. No soft tissue density masses involving the pelvic bones or paraspinal musculature to suggest myeloid sarcoma or chloroma. The sacroiliac joints are unremarkable.

Soft tissues: No abnormal soft tissue masses in the pelvis or perineum.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Complete resolution of the right lower lobe ground-glass opacity previously identified at day +90 post-transplant imaging. The lungs are now clear bilaterally without residual infectious, inflammatory, or neoplastic process.

2. Spleen is normal in size at 9.8 cm, essentially stable compared to the prior examination and markedly reduced from initial presentation, consistent with sustained hematologic response.

3. No evidence of lymphadenopathy above or below the diaphragm.

4. No hepatic lesions. Hepatomegaly noted at initial presentation has remained resolved.

5. No imaging findings to suggest extramedullary leukemic involvement, myeloid sarcoma, or chloroma in any visualized compartment.

6. No pleural effusions, pericardial effusion, or ascites.

7. No suspicious osseous lesions in the visualized skeleton.

Overall, this is a reassuring 6-month post-transplant surveillance examination with no imaging evidence of leukemic disease recurrence or significant post-transplant complication.
